scenarios.scenario: Renderer sends pill mode on collapse
scenarios.when: the renderer calls `window.clui.setViewMode('pill')`
scenarios.then: the main process SHALL call `setBounds()` with width=320, height=48, preserving the anchor point

scenarios.scenario: Renderer sends expanded mode on expand during live phase
scenarios.when: the renderer calls `window.clui.setViewMode('expanded')` during `phase=live`
scenarios.then: the main process SHALL call `setBounds()` with width=560, height=620, preserving the anchor point

scenarios.scenario: Renderer sends full mode for writers room
scenarios.when: the renderer calls `window.clui.setViewMode('full')` during `phase=writers_room`
scenarios.then: the main process SHALL call `setBounds()` with width=560, height=740, preserving the anchor point

scenarios.scenario: Window creation uses pill dimensions by default
scenarios.when: the app first creates the BrowserWindow
scenarios.then: the initial dimensions SHALL NOT use fixed-canvas constants the initial dimensions SHALL be the `full` mode size (560x740) since the app starts in DarkStudioView (expanded=true, phase=no_show)

scenarios.scenario: Pill view has transparent corners
scenarios.when: the window is in pill mode (320x48)
scenarios.then: the rounded-full corners of the PillView SHALL show through to the desktop, not black rectangles

scenarios.scenario: No flash on resize
scenarios.when: the window transitions from pill (320x48) to expanded (560x620)
scenarios.then: the transparent background SHALL prevent any visible flash or black rectangle during the resize

scenarios.scenario: Mouse events reach all UI elements without toggle
scenarios.when: the user moves the cursor over any part of the Showtime window
scenarios.then: mouse events (click, hover, drag) SHALL be received by the renderer without any `setIgnoreMouseEvents` toggling

scenarios.scenario: No mousemove handler on document
scenarios.when: the App component mounts
scenarios.then: no `mousemove` or `mouseleave` event listeners SHALL be registered on `document` for click-through purposes

scenarios.scenario: ExpandedView title bar is draggable via CSS class
scenarios.when: the ExpandedView renders its title bar
scenarios.then: the title bar div SHALL have the `drag-region` class the title bar div SHALL NOT have any inline `style` prop for WebkitAppRegion

scenarios.scenario: Interactive buttons within drag region use no-drag class
scenarios.when: a button (Director, collapse, close) renders inside a drag-region title bar
scenarios.then: the button SHALL have the `no-drag` class the button SHALL NOT have any inline `style` prop for WebkitAppRegion

scenarios.scenario: PillView has no drag region
scenarios.when: the PillView renders
scenarios.then: no element within PillView SHALL have the `drag-region` class or `-webkit-app-region: drag` the entire PillView SHALL remain click-only (clicking expands to ExpandedView)

scenarios.scenario: No view renders data-clui-ui
scenarios.when: any Showtime view renders (DarkStudioView, StrikeView, PillView, ExpandedView, WritersRoomView, OnboardingView)
scenarios.then: no DOM element SHALL have a `data-clui-ui` attribute

scenarios.scenario: Pill-to-expanded preserves bottom-center
scenarios.when: the pill is at screen position where its center-bottom is at (800, 1050)
scenarios.then: after expanding to 560x620, the new bounds SHALL place the center-bottom at (800, 1050) the new x SHALL be 800 - 280 = 520, the new y SHALL be 1050 - 620 = 430

scenarios.scenario: Expanded-to-pill preserves bottom-center
scenarios.when: the expanded view's center-bottom is at (800, 1050)
scenarios.then: after collapsing to pill (320x48), the new bounds SHALL place the center-bottom at (800, 1050) the new x SHALL be 800 - 160 = 640, the new y SHALL be 1050 - 48 = 1002

scenarios.scenario: Anchor updates after user drag
scenarios.when: the user drags the window to a new position
scenarios.then: the stored anchor point SHALL update to the center-bottom of the new position subsequent view mode transitions SHALL use the updated anchor

scenarios.scenario: Window on secondary display with cursor on primary
scenarios.when: the user has dragged the window to a secondary display
scenarios.then: the cursor position SHALL NOT be used for display detection `screen.getDisplayMatching(win.getBounds())` SHALL determine the correct display for clamping

scenarios.scenario: Clamping prevents window from going off-screen
scenarios.when: anchor-based positioning would place the window partially off-screen
scenarios.then: the bounds SHALL be clamped to the display's `workArea` so the entire window remains visible

scenarios.scenario: setViewMode during drag is deferred
scenarios.when: the user is actively dragging the window AND a `setViewMode('pill')` IPC message arrives
scenarios.then: the `setBounds()` call SHALL NOT execute immediately after the drag ends (the `moved` event fires), the deferred `setBounds()` SHALL execute with the correct anchor from the new drag position

scenarios.scenario: Normal setViewMode without drag executes immediately
scenarios.when: the user is NOT dragging the window AND a `setViewMode('expanded')` IPC message arrives
scenarios.then: the `setBounds()` call SHALL execute immediately

scenarios.scenario: Beat check triggers while in pill view
scenarios.when: `beatCheckPending` transitions from false to true AND `isExpanded` is false
scenarios.then: the renderer SHALL set `isExpanded = true` in the showStore the renderer SHALL call `window.clui.setViewMode('expanded')` the BeatCheckModal SHALL render in the 560x620 window

scenarios.scenario: Beat check triggers while already expanded
scenarios.when: `beatCheckPending` transitions from false to true AND `isExpanded` is true
scenarios.then: no view mode change is needed the BeatCheckModal SHALL render normally as an overlay

scenarios.scenario: Window has native shadow
scenarios.when: the BrowserWindow is created
scenarios.then: the `hasShadow` option SHALL be `true`

scenarios.scenario: Window creation on macOS
scenarios.when: `createWindow()` executes on macOS
scenarios.then: the BrowserWindow SHALL have all the listed settings `setWindowButtonPosition` SHALL be called with `{ x: 12, y: 14 }` no `setIgnoreMouseEvents` call SHALL occur in `ready-to-show`

scenarios.scenario: First show after launch centers on cursor display
scenarios.when: `showWindow()` is called for the first time (no stored anchor)
scenarios.then: the window SHALL be positioned on the display nearest the cursor, horizontally centered, bottom-aligned with margin

scenarios.scenario: Subsequent show preserves last anchor
scenarios.when: `showWindow()` is called after the user previously dragged the window
scenarios.then: the window SHALL appear at the stored anchor position, not re-centered on the cursor's display

scenarios.scenario: CLAUDE.md reflects new window management rules
scenarios.when: a contributor reads CLAUDE.md Section 4
scenarios.then: the mandatory BrowserWindow settings SHALL include `hasShadow: true` the mandatory BrowserWindow settings SHALL NOT list `transparent: true` (it is still used but not a rule) the drag region guidance SHALL reference `.drag-region` / `.no-drag` CSS classes there SHALL be a note prohibiting `setIgnoreMouseEvents`
